name: vm-template-creation
description: Create, configure, and manage VM templates in Proxmox. Build reusable VM images for rapid deployment of standardized environments, including Kubernetes clusters and managed applications.
capabilities: Create VM templates from cloud images or custom configurations
Convert configured VMs into reusable templates
Clone templates to deploy multiple standardized VMs
Configure Cloud-Init settings for automated provisioning
Build specialized templates for Kubernetes and applications
author: DataKnifeAI
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation and metadata files. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or file system operations are present. The static scanner flagged generic patterns (e.g., 'main' git branch, 'curl' package names, 'clone' function names) as false positives. All files are safe markdown documentation describing Proxmox VM template workflows.
valueStatement: Building VM templates manually takes significant time and leads to inconsistent configurations. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for creating reusable Proxmox VM templates, enabling standardized infrastructure deployment in minutes instead of hours.
userTitle: Create Proxmox VM templates for rapid deployment
limitations: Templates cannot be booted directly (must be cloned first)
Large templates require more time to clone across the cluster
Network configuration occurs post-clone via Cloud-Init
Disk image import requires manual Proxmox CLI steps

useCases.title: Standardize Kubernetes Infrastructure
useCases.description: Build RKE2-ready Ubuntu templates for consistent Kubernetes cluster deployment across development and production environments.

useCases.title: Rapid VM Provisioning
useCases.description: Create application-specific templates to deploy identical servers in minutes for web, database, and CI/CD workloads.

useCases.title: Maintain IaC Consistency
useCases.description: Develop versioned templates with documented configurations to ensure infrastructure consistency across the organization.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Template Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a template VM named ubuntu-22.04-template on node pve2 with 2 CPU cores, 4GB memory, and 50GB disk for Cloud-Init deployment.

promptTemplates.title: Mark VM as Template
promptTemplates.prompt: Mark VM 100 on pve2 as a template so it can be cloned for deployments.

promptTemplates.title: Kubernetes Cluster Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Clone ubuntu-rke2-template (VM 100) three times to create control plane nodes named rancher-1, rancher-2, and rancher-3 with full clone mode.

promptTemplates.title: Template Update Workflow
promptTemplates.scenario: Update existing template with new configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Clone template VM 100 to VM 900 as a test instance, then guide me through updating it with Docker support and security patches before creating a new versioned template.

bestPractices: Use Cloud-Init for network and hostname configuration to keep templates minimal and portable
Apply security hardening and patches before marking a VM as a template
Document template versions, contents, and creation dates for audit and reproducibility
antiPatterns: Booting templates directly instead of cloning them first
Leaving sensitive data or credentials on VMs before converting to templates
Using thick provisioning for templates when thin provisioning provides better storage efficiency

faq.question: What Proxmox versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill works with Proxmox VE 7.x and 8.x. MCP tools require API access to your Proxmox node.

faq.question: How many VMs can I clone from a template?
faq.answer: There is no limit on clone count. Each clone is an independent VM with its own resources.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with Terraform?
faq.answer: Yes. Use MCP tools interactively for template preparation, then Terraform for production-scale cloning.

faq.question: Is my data safe when creating templates?
faq.answer: This skill only generates configuration guidance. Actual VM operations are performed by Proxmox MCP tools on your infrastructure.

faq.question: Why do my cloned VMs have the same IP address?
faq.answer: Templates store initial network config. Use Cloud-Init with DHCP or unique static IPs per clone for proper networking.

faq.question: How is this different from Terraform Proxmox provider?
faq.answer: Terraform manages infrastructure state at scale. This skill provides interactive guidance for template design and testing.
